provider_option_rows.provider_label: Self-hosted OTLP collector + trace / metric / log stack
provider_option_rows.topology_summary: Keep collector, traces, metrics, logs, security, and privacy stores inside the first-party platform boundary.
provider_option_rows.backend_summary: Portable baseline that satisfies first-party retention and access-control law without delegating primary telemetry truth to a vendor.
provider_option_rows.privacy_summary: Most direct fit for strict redaction, restricted access slices, and replay-safe joins to audit evidence.
notes: Strongest alignment with the corpus' first-party telemetry posture.
Introduces operator burden for collector, storage, and scaling operations.

source_refs.rationale: Operational, security, and privacy telemetry must remain distinct from append-only audit evidence.
Every signal family needs correlation keys that bind telemetry back to manifest, authority, workflow, and release lineage.
Compliance-capable traces must preserve manifest-rooted structure and explicit sampling posture for critical flows.
Reliability, security, privacy, and delivery metrics are mandatory even when backend product choice remains open.
Structured logging must include correlation keys and message-template posture while excluding raw secrets and sensitive bodies.
Sampling and retention decisions for telemetry may never erase required audit history or collapse restricted event families into generic logs.
Critical spans, authority mutations, and blocking gates require explicit trace and audit linkage rather than best-effort telemetry.
Visibility classes, masking posture, and retention state have to survive across the telemetry and audit split.
Telemetry secrets, headers, and downstream credentials must stay in governed secret boundaries and out of logs or repo-tracked payloads.
The runtime topology already separates operator access, orchestrator, workers, authority gateway, broker, control store, and audit store, so telemetry topology must match those boundaries.
Release correlation requires build digest, candidate identity, schema bundle, and admissibility lineage inside telemetry resources and joins.
The Sentry-compatible monitoring workspace already exists as a secondary vendor overlay and must not be mistaken for the primary telemetry substrate.
The dependency register keeps core OpenTelemetry backends in procurement or platform-choice posture, so this card must stay portable and fail closed.

provider_option_rows.topology_summary: Use the eventual platform provider's OTLP-capable tracing, metrics, and logging stores behind the same gateway pattern.
provider_option_rows.backend_summary: Works only after the platform decision from the adjacent infrastructure cards is frozen.
provider_option_rows.privacy_summary: Requires explicit restricted-dataset and vendor-export disablement posture for security and privacy signals.
notes: This row is blocked until the broader platform provider is chosen.

provider_option_rows.provider_label: Managed OTLP search / metric stack with first-party gateway
provider_option_rows.topology_summary: Keep the gateway and redaction in first party, then export to a managed backend that accepts OTLP or compatible transformed signals.
provider_option_rows.backend_summary: Viable only if the managed backend can preserve first-party restricted lanes and explicit retention classes.
provider_option_rows.privacy_summary: Needs additional governance review before any security or privacy signal can leave the platform boundary.
notes: Permitted only for signal families explicitly marked vendor-exportable.

collector_tier_rows.label: Direct SDK OTLP clients
collector_tier_rows.deployment_scope: Browser operator shell, customer portal shell, native macOS workspace, and lightweight services with safe direct export posture.
collector_tier_rows.resilience_posture: Bounded batch and timeout at the SDK edge; local browser or native caches never become legal telemetry truth.
notes: Direct SDK paths are appropriate where a workload-local agent is unavailable or unnecessary.

collector_tier_rows.label: Workload-local agent collectors
collector_tier_rows.deployment_scope: Manifest orchestrator, stage workers, authority gateway, and projector workloads running on platform hosts or nodes.
collector_tier_rows.ingress_mode: Local OTLP receive with host or workload resource enrichment
collector_tier_rows.resilience_posture: Bounded local queue plus optional WAL before forwarding to the environment gateway; no durable truth written here.
notes: Agent tier absorbs local bursts and normalizes resource identity before cross-network export.

collector_tier_rows.label: Environment gateway collectors
collector_tier_rows.deployment_scope: One environment-scoped gateway tier per stable runtime namespace and one isolated gateway for ephemeral preview.
collector_tier_rows.ingress_mode: Single environment OTLP ingress for direct SDKs and workload agents
collector_tier_rows.resilience_posture: Bounded sending queues, retry with backoff, persistent WAL for remote exporters, and failure-aware degraded mode that protects runtime correctness.
notes: Tail sampling and cross-signal routing happen at the gateway so policy remains centralized and environment-scoped.

collector_tier_rows.label: Vendor overlay bridge
collector_tier_rows.deployment_scope: Exporter lane inside the environment gateway that forwards only allowlisted trace envelopes, errors, and release markers to the adopted Sentry-compatible workspace.
collector_tier_rows.ingress_mode: Collector exporter bridge only; never a primary ingest endpoint
collector_tier_rows.resilience_posture: Non-blocking exporter with independent queue and redaction gate; vendor outage cannot fail the product or suppress audit capture.
notes: Secondary-only overlay from pc_0044 for grouped diagnostics and release markers.

backend_rows.access_posture: restricted runtime and incident-response operators only
backend_rows.storage_class: hot query window plus short warm retention

signal_rows.label: Audit links
signal_rows.lineage_strip_label: Join metadata -> first-party join index -> control and audit stores -> deterministic investigation frames
notes: Join indices improve investigation speed but remain rebuildable from durable truth.

source_refs.rationale: Operational, security, and privacy telemetry must remain distinct from append-only audit evidence.
Every signal family needs correlation keys that bind telemetry back to manifest, authority, workflow, and release lineage.
Compliance-capable traces must preserve manifest-rooted structure and explicit sampling posture for critical flows.
Reliability, security, privacy, and delivery metrics are mandatory even when backend product choice remains open.
Structured logging must include correlation keys and message-template posture while excluding raw secrets and sensitive bodies.
Sampling and retention decisions for telemetry may never erase required audit history or collapse restricted event families into generic logs.
Critical spans, authority mutations, and blocking gates require explicit trace and audit linkage rather than best-effort telemetry.
Visibility classes, masking posture, and retention state have to survive across the telemetry and audit split.
Telemetry secrets, headers, and downstream credentials must stay in governed secret boundaries and out of logs or repo-tracked payloads.
The runtime topology already separates operator access, orchestrator, workers, authority gateway, broker, control store, and audit store, so telemetry topology must match those boundaries.
Release correlation requires build digest, candidate identity, schema bundle, and admissibility lineage inside telemetry resources and joins.
The Sentry-compatible monitoring workspace already exists as a secondary vendor overlay and must not be mistaken for the primary telemetry substrate.
The dependency register keeps core OpenTelemetry backends in procurement or platform-choice posture, so this card must stay portable and fail closed.

truth_boundary_statement: OpenTelemetry collection is the first-party observability substrate. The Sentry-compatible workspace from pc_0044 remains a secondary overlay and never replaces audit, release evidence, or restricted telemetry.
notes: The catalog freezes signal routing, backend ownership boundaries, and vendor exportability before concrete backend products are chosen.
Security, privacy, and audit-link lanes remain first-party only.
